assumption: Varying case study structure per project type (enterprise vs. startup) reads as more senior than using the same template for all.
evidence_for: COMP-001: senior portfolios vary structure. Junior signal: follows the same template for every case study. Tobias van Schneider's analysis confirms.
evidence_against: Consistency in structure makes the portfolio feel cohesive and reduces cognitive load for the reader.
test: Get feedback from 3-5 hiring managers on whether the current uniform structure feels junior.
